pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to automate checking customer loyalty status in a legacy green-screen system with no APIs. What is the most appropriate primary technology?
opcoes: MuleSoft Composer calling a REST API
justificativa: MuleSoft RPA é a tecnologia ideal para sistemas legados sem APIs através de automação de tela.
correta_texto: MuleSoft RPA with screen automation

pergunta: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) wants to quickly sync standard objects between Salesforce and NetSuite using clicks, not code. Which solution best fits the requirement?
opcoes: Anypoint Studio with custom DataWeave transformations
MuleSoft RPA recording user actions in both systems
MuleSoft Composer with prebuilt SaaS connectors
Salesforce Flow with Scheduled Path and custom Apex
justificativa: MuleSoft Composer permite integrações rápidas entre sistemas SaaS usando conectores pré-configurados.
correta_texto: MuleSoft Composer with prebuilt SaaS connectors

pergunta: A company needs to process millions of transaction records nightly, aggregating data from multiple databases. Which component should be the core of this integration?
opcoes: MuleSoft RPA with multiple bots in parallel
MuleSoft Composer flows triggered every 5 minutes
Anypoint Platform APIs with batch processing
Salesforce Flow invoked by Platform Events only
justificativa: Anypoint Platform é a escolha certa para volumes massivos de dados e processamento em lote.
correta_texto: Anypoint Platform APIs with batch processing

pergunta: A project team wants to avoid building multiple logins to the same legacy ERP for different automations. What is the best practice aligned with C4E?
opcoes: Embed login logic inside every bot individually
Reuse a shared RPA process published in Anypoint Exchange
Implement login logic in a Salesforce Screen Flow
Configure separate credentials in each Composer flow
justificativa: O reuso de processos RPA através do Anypoint Exchange promove eficiência e governança alinhada ao C4E.
correta_texto: Reuse a shared RPA process published in Anypoint Exchange

pergunta: A customer asks: "We need the fastest no-code way to connect Salesforce and ServiceNow with simple field mappings." Which two dimensions from the tool selection matrix are most relevant to justify using Composer?
justificativa: Complexidade baixa e perfil de usuário de negócio são os principais fatores para escolher o Composer.
correta_texto: Complexity and User Profile

pergunta: NTO wants to minimize the impact of a flaky on-premises database during request/response lookups in Anypoint APIs. What is the most efficient way to protect the consumer experience?
opcoes: Use RPA to read database screens instead of APIs
Implement exponential backoff and retries in the API
Move all logic into Salesforce Flows
Call the database directly from Composer on every request
justificativa: Backoff exponencial e retentativas aumentam a resiliência de APIs contra falhas temporárias.
correta_texto: Implement exponential backoff and retries in the API

pergunta: A solution architect wants to ensure end-to-end testing of a new hyperautomation scenario before the legacy system is ready. Which component should be used to simulate backend APIs?
opcoes: Salesforce Sandbox with only partial data
justificativa: O Mocking Service do Anypoint permite simular respostas de APIs antes mesmo delas serem implementadas.
correta_texto: Anypoint Exchange Mocking Service

pergunta: In a complex flow involving Salesforce, RPA and Composer, one transaction failed. Logs show that the robot could not open a desktop window. How should the error be classified?
opcoes: Technical exception in the RPA layer
justificativa: Falhas de infraestrutura ou interface no robô são classificadas como exceções técnicas.
correta_texto: Technical exception in the RPA layer

pergunta: A retail company already exposes a stable Customer System API on Anypoint. A new chatbot experience must reuse this data. What is the most efficient way to design the solution?
opcoes: Build a new System API specifically for the chatbot
Call the database directly from Einstein Bot
Create an Experience API on top of the existing System API
Use RPA to query the database with a headless client
justificativa: Seguindo o API-led, deve-se criar uma Experience API que consome as System APIs já existentes.
correta_texto: Create an Experience API on top of the existing System API

pergunta: A bank wants to avoid duplicate processing if a request message is accidentally sent twice to an API that triggers RPA. Which concept is most important here?
justificativa: Idempotência garante que múltiplas chamadas idênticas produzam o mesmo resultado sem processamento duplicado.
correta_texto: Idempotency of API operations

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to automate a stable, repetitive back‑office process currently executed by an operator in a legacy Windows application. There is no API available. What is the most appropriate first technology choice?
opcoes: MuleSoft RPA Builder criando a UI automation process
justificativa: RPA Builder é a ferramenta certa para automatizar interfaces de usuário em sistemas legados Windows.
correta_texto: MuleSoft RPA Builder criando a UI automation process

pergunta: Northern Trail Outfitters (NTO) wants to decide whether a candidate process is suitable for RPA. Which characteristic most strongly indicates a good fit?
opcoes: Process changes its steps every week
Process is rule‑based, high volume and stable over time
Process is performed once per year by executives
justificativa: Processos baseados em regras, estáveis e de alto volume são os melhores candidatos para RPA.
correta_texto: Process is rule‑based, high volume and stable over time

pergunta: A question describes the Evaluation phase inside RPA Manager. What is the most efficient way to use this phase according to best practices?
opcoes: To configure attended robots on users’ desktops
To estimate automation potential and business value before building
To deploy processes directly to production workers
justificativa: A fase de Evaluation no RPA Manager serve para validar o valor de negócio e o potencial de automação.
correta_texto: To estimate automation potential and business value before building

pergunta: In an end‑to‑end test of a hyperautomation flow, which is the most efficient way to isolate where an error occurred?
opcoes: Disable logs and enable only monitoring dashboards
Test the entire chain only from the user interface
Validate RPA, integration APIs and target systems independently
Retry the entire flow repeatedly until it passes
correta_texto: Validate RPA, integration APIs and target systems independently

pergunta: A process involves a long‑running human approval plus a short, technical API call. When designing the architecture, which tool should handle the long‑running orchestration?
correta_texto: Salesforce Flow Orchestration

pergunta: A candidate proposes putting all heavy data transformations into System APIs to keep Process APIs “thin”. Why is this not aligned with MuleSoft best practices?
opcoes: System APIs must not connect to any databases
Process APIs are responsible for orchestration and business logic
RPA bots should handle transformations instead
correta_texto: Process APIs are responsible for orchestration and business logic

pergunta: Which option best describes API‑led Connectivity in the context of hyperautomation?
opcoes: Direct point‑to‑point integrations between all systems
A layered model with System, Process and Experience APIs promoting reuse
A set of UI flows built only in Salesforce
A network of ungoverned webhooks between services
correta_texto: A layered model with System, Process and Experience APIs promoting reuse

pergunta: During an exam question, a scenario offers four valid‑sounding options. Which one is most likely correct according to MuleSoft strategy?
opcoes: The option that recreates existing integrations from scratch
The option that maximizes reuse of existing APIs and assets
The option that adds the greatest number of new components
The option that ignores API‑led layers for speed
correta_texto: The option that maximizes reuse of existing APIs and assets

pergunta: AnyAirlines already has several point‑to‑point integrations between SaaS systems. They now want a more governed and reusable architecture. Which approach best aligns with MuleSoft recommendations?
opcoes: Add more scheduled jobs between systems as needed
Replace all integrations with a single monolithic API
Implement API‑led connectivity with System, Process and Experience APIs
Move all integrations into Salesforce Flow only
correta_texto: Implement API‑led connectivity with System, Process and Experience APIs

pergunta: NTO wants to expose product prices to multiple channels (web, mobile app, Salesforce). What is the most efficient way to design this according to API‑led principles?
opcoes: One Process API that all channels call directly
Separate System APIs for each channel
A shared Product System API plus dedicated Experience APIs per channel
Multiple duplicated APIs per consumer team
correta_texto: A shared Product System API plus dedicated Experience APIs per channel

pergunta: A scenario describes a Composer flow calling an API that performs heavy aggregations across systems. Where should the complex business logic primarily reside?
opcoes: Inside the Salesforce Flow screen logic
Inside a Process API on Anypoint Platform
correta_texto: Inside a Process API on Anypoint Platform

pergunta: A candidate suggests using RPA to automate a system that already exposes robust, secure REST APIs. Which statement best reflects MuleSoft best practice?
opcoes: Acceptable, because RPA is always simpler to configure
Not ideal; prefer API‑based integration first and reserve RPA for no‑API cases
Required, because exam scenarios always prefer RPA
Required, because APIs cannot be reused in other channels
correta_texto: Not ideal; prefer API‑based integration first and reserve RPA for no‑API cases

pergunta: A retail customer wants near real‑time inventory updates between their ERP and Salesforce. Which tool combination best balances speed and maintainability?
opcoes: Only RPA bots reading ERP screens
Anypoint Platform System API for ERP plus Composer for light orchestration
Composer polling ERP database directly every second
correta_texto: Anypoint Platform System API for ERP plus Composer for light orchestration

pergunta: A scenario describes a complex multi‑step process where a human underwriter reviews cases over several days. Which component should coordinate long‑running steps while still leveraging APIs and RPA where needed?
opcoes: Single large RPA process with embedded decision logic
Salesforce Flow Orchestration with background and interactive steps
A large Composer flow with nested If/Else branches
A single Process API with many synchronous calls
correta_texto: Salesforce Flow Orchestration with background and interactive steps

pergunta: In a hyperautomation project, who is primarily responsible for promoting reuse of assets like APIs, RPA processes and fragments according to the C4E model?
opcoes: The Center for Enablement acting as an enablement and governance team
correta_texto: The Center for Enablement acting as an enablement and governance team

pergunta: A question describes repeated failures in a hyperautomation chain whenever a legacy system is under heavy load. Which combination best addresses resilience at the integration layer?
opcoes: Remove logs to save processing time
Implement retries with exponential backoff and timeouts in the APIs
Move all logic to RPA bots to hide the problem
correta_texto: Implement retries with exponential backoff and timeouts in the APIs

pergunta: A company wants to test a new orchestration between Salesforce Flow and RPA while the final production credentials are not yet available. What is the safest test data strategy?
opcoes: Use real customer data in production systems
Use synthetic/mock data defined in API specifications
Export CSVs from production and mask only names
Duplicate production environment without any anonymization
correta_texto: Use synthetic/mock data defined in API specifications

pergunta: The exam presents four tool choices for a simple SaaS‑to‑SaaS integration with low volume and business admin ownership. Which one is most aligned with MuleSoft guidance?
opcoes: Anypoint Platform with fully custom APIs
RPA bots simulating all user clicks
correta_texto: MuleSoft Composer with out‑of‑the‑box connectors

pergunta: An architect proposes to centralize all error handling in the RPA layer. Why is this approach problematic for hyperautomation?
opcoes: APIs never fail, so this is unnecessary
Each layer (APIs, RPA, Flow, Composer) should handle its own technical concerns
Composer has no way to react to failures
correta_texto: Each layer (APIs, RPA, Flow, Composer) should handle its own technical concerns

pergunta: A scenario mentions “avoiding duplicate login implementations across multiple teams”. Which platform capability directly supports this goal?
opcoes: Publishing shared RPA processes and API specs in Anypoint Exchange
Creating separate logins in each project for independence
Storing credentials in local text files
correta_texto: Publishing shared RPA processes and API specs in Anypoint Exchange

pergunta: NTO wants to quickly pilot a new customer journey using mocked APIs while backend systems are still under design. Which combination best supports this parallel development?
opcoes: Anypoint API Designer + Mocking Service consumed by Flow/Composer
Manual JSON files exchanged over email
Only Salesforce custom objects with no external connectivity
correta_texto: Anypoint API Designer + Mocking Service consumed by Flow/Composer

pergunta: A question describes a flow where Salesforce Screen Flows collect data from agents, which is then processed by RPA in a legacy system. Where should the primary user experience be built?
opcoes: In Salesforce Screen Flows integrated with APIs/RPA
correta_texto: In Salesforce Screen Flows integrated with APIs/RPA

pergunta: An exam item shows multiple design options. Which is the strongest sign that one option is a “trap” rather than the recommended architecture?
opcoes: It emphasizes reuse of existing APIs and assets
It replaces three integrations with a single well‑designed Process API
It adds unnecessary components and ignores existing reusable services
It mentions C4E and governance practices
correta_texto: It adds unnecessary components and ignores existing reusable services

pergunta: AnyAirlines wants to expose flight status to partners, mobile apps and internal portals. What is the most efficient high‑level design?
opcoes: One monolithic API mixing database calls and UI logic
Separate, duplicated APIs for each consumer
System APIs for core data plus Process and Experience APIs for each channel
Only RPA bots reading from the reservation system
correta_texto: System APIs for core data plus Process and Experience APIs for each channel

pergunta: A scenario offers the option to “rebuild a Customer System API from scratch” even though one already exists. Why is this typically wrong?
opcoes: It violates the principle of reuse and increases maintenance
System APIs are not allowed to expose customer data
correta_texto: It violates the principle of reuse and increases maintenance

pergunta: NTO needs a quick proof‑of‑concept integration from Salesforce to a legacy mainframe with no APIs. Which answer best reflects MuleSoft guidance?
opcoes: Start with MuleSoft RPA to simulate user interactions
Wait until a new REST API is built before doing anything
Use Composer directly against the database tables
correta_texto: Start with MuleSoft RPA to simulate user interactions

pergunta: In an exam question, one option suggests performing complex aggregations in the Experience API, while another suggests doing it in the Process API. Which is more aligned with API‑led?
opcoes: Experience API, because it is closest to the user
Process API, because it centralizes business logic and orchestration
Both are equivalent according to MuleSoft
Neither; logic must always be in System APIs
correta_texto: Process API, because it centralizes business logic and orchestration

pergunta: A flow uses RPA to fetch data from a legacy claims system and then sends the result to Salesforce. Where should retries and timeouts for unstable connectivity primarily be configured?
opcoes: In the integration/API layer that receives or calls the RPA process
correta_texto: In the integration/API layer that receives or calls the RPA process

pergunta: A candidate chooses an option that adds Salesforce Flow Orchestration plus multiple new APIs when a simple synchronous API call from a Screen Flow would suffice. What kind of trap is this?
correta_texto: Alternative that adds unnecessary complexity

pergunta: An exam item describes a need for one‑time data migration from a CSV file into Salesforce. Which is the most appropriate integration approach?
opcoes: Full API‑led architecture with three layers
Complex RPA automation mimicking user input
Native Salesforce data import tools or simple integration
Building long‑running orchestrations with many stages
correta_texto: Native Salesforce data import tools or simple integration

pergunta: A scenario gives two options: call a Process API that orchestrates multiple System APIs, or let Composer directly call each underlying system separately. Which is typically preferred?
opcoes: Composer calling all systems directly for flexibility
A Process API orchestrating System APIs, reused by Composer and other clients
Only RPA calling each system sequentially
correta_texto: A Process API orchestrating System APIs, reused by Composer and other clients

pergunta: A hyperautomation design includes Composer, RPA, Flow and multiple APIs. Which guiding principle helps keep this architecture maintainable?
opcoes: Push every responsibility to a single tool
Use each tool for the role it is strongest at and maximize reuse
Duplicate logic in each layer for redundancy
Prefer UI automation over APIs whenever possible
correta_texto: Use each tool for the role it is strongest at and maximize reuse

pergunta: An option claims that “creating separate APIs for each consuming application” improves reuse. Why is this misleading?
opcoes: MuleSoft does not support multiple APIs
True reuse comes from generic System/Process APIs with Experience APIs only when needed
Reuse is unrelated to API design
correta_texto: True reuse comes from generic System/Process APIs with Experience APIs only when needed

pergunta: A company wants to validate that their architecture still follows best practices one year after go‑live. Which practice best supports this?
opcoes: Ignoring C4E and allowing each team to evolve independently
Reviewing assets published in Anypoint Exchange and enforcing reuse policies
correta_texto: Reviewing assets published in Anypoint Exchange and enforcing reuse policies

pergunta: In a multi‑choice question, two options look good, but one ignores API‑led layers and directly couples front‑end to databases. How should this influence the choice?
opcoes: Prefer the option that respects API‑led separation, even if more subtle
Prefer the option that bypasses layers for performance
Choose randomly if both seem possible
correta_texto: Prefer the option that respects API‑led separation, even if more subtle

pergunta: A scenario describes a business user who must maintain a simple integration without developer support. Which integration tool is the exam most likely to expect?
opcoes: Anypoint Studio with complex DataWeave scripts
correta_texto: MuleSoft Composer with guided interface

pergunta: NTO needs a fast temporary workaround using RPA while the proper APIs are being built. Which statement aligns with best practice?
opcoes: RPA should permanently replace API‑based integrations
RPA can act as a bridge, but long‑term strategy should be API‑led
RPA and APIs should never coexist
APIs are only for external consumers
correta_texto: RPA can act as a bridge, but long‑term strategy should be API‑led

pergunta: An exam option describes “calling the same System API from multiple Experience APIs and from RPA/Composer flows”. How should this be interpreted?
opcoes: As an anti‑pattern that must be avoided
As strong evidence of reuse and correct layering
As a sign that the System API is too generic
As a violation of security principles
correta_texto: As strong evidence of reuse and correct layering

pergunta: A company wants traceability of who approved which bot process and when it went to production. Which RPA Manager capability is most relevant?
opcoes: Lifecycle with stages like Evaluation, Design, Build, Test, Production
correta_texto: Lifecycle with stages like Evaluation, Design, Build, Test, Production

pergunta: A scenario describes a new process that is still being redesigned by the business team and frequently changes the system steps. What is the best recommendation?
opcoes: Immediately automate with RPA to capture all changes
Wait until the process is stable before committing to RPA
Use RPA only in production, not in test
Replace the process with manual spreadsheets permanently
correta_texto: Wait until the process is stable before committing to RPA

pergunta: NTO needs to ensure that passwords used by bots are never visible to RPA developers. Which component should be highlighted in the answer?
opcoes: Local configuration files on the bot machine
correta_texto: Credential Manager in RPA Manager

pergunta: A candidate solution stores credentials in an Excel file read by the bot at runtime. Why is this not aligned with MuleSoft RPA best practices?
opcoes: Credentials must be stored securely and centrally in Credential Manager
RPA Manager does not allow bots to access files
correta_texto: Credentials must be stored securely and centrally in Credential Manager

pergunta: A bank wants to run multiple independent RPA processes in parallel on the same infrastructure to maximize ROI. Which concept is most relevant?
opcoes: RPA Agents configured to handle multiple processes
correta_texto: RPA Agents configured to handle multiple processes

pergunta: A scenario says: “The bot must run even when no human is logged into Windows and no monitor is attached.” Which feature enables this behavior?
opcoes: Secure Session on the RPA Agent
Only attended robots launched by users
correta_texto: Secure Session on the RPA Agent

pergunta: A process is being moved from Test to Production in RPA Manager. What is the primary expectation before switching the lifecycle stage?
opcoes: The process has at least one failure to validate logs
The process executed successfully in a controlled test environment
The process is triggered only once in sandbox
correta_texto: The process executed successfully in a controlled test environment

pergunta: NTO wants to analyze savings and business impact of RPA automations. Which RPA Manager capability best supports this?
opcoes: Dashboard de Análise (ROI and time savings)
correta_texto: Dashboard de Análise (ROI and time savings)

pergunta: A candidate suggests using Composer instead of RPA for a legacy desktop app with no APIs but simple, low‑volume usage. Which argument best supports choosing RPA instead?
opcoes: Composer does not support any SaaS connectors
RPA is the only tool that can interact via UI when no API exists
RPA is always cheaper than Composer
correta_texto: RPA is the only tool that can interact via UI when no API exists

pergunta: A scenario describes that business wants non‑technical users to monitor the health of bots and restart failed items. Which interface should they primarily use?
correta_texto: RPA Manager – Process Operations

pergunta: A process candidate has extremely low volume but high financial impact and is performed monthly. What is the most appropriate guidance for the exam?
opcoes: Automatically reject it as an RPA candidate
Consider RPA only if the process is complex but stable and rules‑based
Only use RPA for daily processes
Only Composer can handle monthly scenarios
correta_texto: Consider RPA only if the process is complex but stable and rules‑based

pergunta: A company wants to avoid building multiple RPA processes that log into the same system with different credentials and logic. Which practice best aligns with C4E and reuse?
opcoes: Duplicate login logic in every bot for flexibility
Publish a shared login automation as an RPA asset in Exchange
Force all teams to build their own logins from scratch
Move all logins to unmanaged scripts outside RPA
correta_texto: Publish a shared login automation as an RPA asset in Exchange

pergunta: AnyAirlines is evaluating several candidate processes for RPA. Which combination best fits the Evaluation phase output in RPA Manager?
opcoes: A list of UI selectors for each button
A scorecard showing automation fitness and expected ROI
A deployment package for production agents
A log of all bot executions
correta_texto: A scorecard showing automation fitness and expected ROI

pergunta: A scenario lists the following order: Build → Evaluation → Production → Test. What is the correct lifecycle sequence in MuleSoft RPA?
correta_texto: Evaluation → Design → Build → Test → Production

pergunta: NTO wants to ensure that the workflow implemented in Builder matches the business process diagram. Which standard and phase are most relevant?
opcoes: BPMN diagram created in the Design phase
Swagger definition imported into RPA Manager
correta_texto: BPMN diagram created in the Design phase

pergunta: A candidate solution jumps directly from Recorder output to Production without modifications. Why is this generally wrong?
opcoes: Recorder already optimizes selectors for all systems
Recorder only generates a skeleton and requires refinement in Builder
correta_texto: Recorder only generates a skeleton and requires refinement in Builder

pergunta: A process automates a web application that changes CSS classes frequently but keeps HTML IDs stable. What is the recommended selector strategy?
opcoes: Use stable object‑based selectors like IDs or robust XPaths
Use random wait times and screen coordinates
correta_texto: Use stable object‑based selectors like IDs or robust XPaths

pergunta: A legacy desktop app has no accessible object model and uses fixed‑position buttons. Which two techniques are most likely for RPA to interact with it?
opcoes: Direct SQL queries to the backend database
correta_texto: Image‑based recognition and coordinate‑based clicks

pergunta: A scenario describes OCR being used to read text from scanned PDFs before entering data into an ERP. Where does this capability typically live in MuleSoft RPA?
opcoes: In Builder activities that support OCR engines
correta_texto: In Builder activities that support OCR engines

pergunta: During testing, a bot fails when a desktop window takes longer than usual to appear. Which adjustment is most appropriate?
opcoes: Disable all waits to speed execution
Increase timeout and implement retries with screenshots on failure
Run the bot only during off‑hours
correta_texto: Increase timeout and implement retries with screenshots on failure

pergunta: NTO wants to distinguish between technical errors (window not found) and business exceptions (invalid ID) in reports. How should the process be designed?
opcoes: Treat all errors as business exceptions
Use dedicated error handling blocks and raise specific business exceptions for data issues
Ignore exceptions and rely on logs only
Let the operating system classify errors automatically
correta_texto: Use dedicated error handling blocks and raise specific business exceptions for data issues

pergunta: A process frequently fails because the target application was updated and a button label changed slightly. Which practice best minimizes future maintenance effort?
opcoes: Use generic selectors based on window position only
Use robust selectors focusing on stable attributes instead of visible text
Re‑record the entire process after every minor change
Abort automation and switch to manual processing
correta_texto: Use robust selectors focusing on stable attributes instead of visible text

pergunta: A candidate suggests executing RPA bots only in attended mode on users’ desktops for all critical processes. Which downside does the exam expect you to recognize?
opcoes: Attended mode cannot use Credential Manager
Attended bots cannot access web applications
It reduces scalability and breaks the idea of fully automated, scheduled runs
It removes the ability to log errors
correta_texto: It reduces scalability and breaks the idea of fully automated, scheduled runs

pergunta: A banking process must run at night without any human present, accessing multiple secure systems. Which combination is most aligned with best practices?
opcoes: Attended bots with passwords typed by operators
Secure Session plus Credential Manager with scheduled execution
Local scripts storing passwords in plain text
correta_texto: Secure Session plus Credential Manager with scheduled execution

pergunta: A scenario describes heavy reuse of the same sub‑workflow across several RPA processes (for example, “open SAP session”). How should this be managed?
opcoes: Duplicate the activities in every process
Create a reusable component and share it as an RPA asset
Implement it separately in each bot without documentation
Move this logic fully into Salesforce Flow
correta_texto: Create a reusable component and share it as an RPA asset

pergunta: A candidate process is selected mainly because it uses many different systems and seems “interesting” for RPA developers, but it has low volume and unclear rules. What is the recommended decision?
opcoes: Approve immediately because complexity justifies RPA
Reject or postpone until the process is standardized and value is clear
Implement only half of the steps
Split it randomly across many bots
correta_texto: Reject or postpone until the process is standardized and value is clear

pergunta: A question contrasts “record‑and‑playback only” with “structured workflow designed in Builder using BPMN concepts and parameters”. Which option better represents a production‑ready approach?
opcoes: Record‑and‑playback only, because it is faster
Structured Builder workflow aligned with BPMN design and parameterized data
Only scripting in external tools, without RPA platform
correta_texto: Structured Builder workflow aligned with BPMN design and parameterized data

pergunta: AnyAirlines reports that a bot suddenly stopped clicking the “Save” button in a web app after a browser update. What is the most appropriate first action?
opcoes: Rebuild the entire RPA process from scratch
Update the selector (XPath/locator) in RPA Builder and republish
correta_texto: Update the selector (XPath/locator) in RPA Builder and republish

pergunta: An exam question describes “CPF inválido” being detected by the bot when validating input data. How should this failure be classified?
correta_texto: Business exception

pergunta: A bot cannot find a window because the application server is down. What is the most appropriate handling?
opcoes: Treat as business exception and send to human review queue
Treat as technical exception and implement retry with exponential backoff
Ignore the error and continue processing
Ask the end‑user to fix it manually every time
correta_texto: Treat as technical exception and implement retry with exponential backoff

pergunta: NTO wants to provide detailed evidence when a technical error occurs in production. Which design choice best supports troubleshooting?
opcoes: Capture screenshots and key context in error handling blocks
Store errors only in local text files on the bot machine
Send only a generic email without details
correta_texto: Capture screenshots and key context in error handling blocks

pergunta: A scenario describes bots using shared Windows accounts that multiple humans also know. Why is this problematic from an exam perspective?
opcoes: It prevents bots from running in Secure Session
It violates security and auditability; credentials should be managed centrally and not shared
It makes bots faster than allowed
It is required for Credential Manager
correta_texto: It violates security and auditability; credentials should be managed centrally and not shared

pergunta: A financial institution requires that no one, including developers, knows the actual passwords used by bots. Which RPA Manager feature should be emphasized?
correta_texto: Credential Manager with role‑based access

pergunta: During testing, a bot encounters invalid business data and raises a business exception, sending the case to a human analyst. What is the main benefit of this pattern?
opcoes: Hides technical problems from business users
Ensures that data issues are routed to the right human decision point
Guarantees that no manual work is ever needed
correta_texto: Ensures that data issues are routed to the right human decision point

pergunta: A scenario states that multiple RPA processes must be moved from Test to Production while maintaining clear separation of environments. What is a recommended practice?
opcoes: Use the same credentials and endpoints in all stages
Configure distinct environments and lifecycles for Test and Production in RPA Manager
Run Test and Production on the same Windows session
correta_texto: Configure distinct environments and lifecycles for Test and Production in RPA Manager

pergunta: NTO sees that some robots occasionally fail due to temporary network issues. Which combination best aligns with robust error handling?
opcoes: Immediate permanent failure on first error
Retry with exponential backoff and escalation if threshold is exceeded
Manual restart only, without any automation
correta_texto: Retry with exponential backoff and escalation if threshold is exceeded

pergunta: An exam option suggests that “Secure Session requires a human to stay logged into Windows”. Why is this option incorrect?
opcoes: Secure Session is only for Linux

pergunta: Uma alternativa diz que “Background Steps não podem chamar APIs, apenas atualizar campos locais”. Com base no conteúdo, isso é:
opcoes: Falso; Background Steps executam Flows, que podem chamar APIs MuleSoft e outros serviços
correta_texto: Falso; Background Steps executam Flows, que podem chamar APIs MuleSoft e outros serviços

pergunta: NTO quer entender quem está segurando um processo parado. Que combinação de informações a Orchestration fornece?
opcoes: Orchestration Runs + Work Items, mostrando em qual Step/usuário/queue o processo está
correta_texto: Orchestration Runs + Work Items, mostrando em qual Step/usuário/queue o processo está

pergunta: AnyAirlines tem um processo de compras: funcionário pede item, gerente aprova, API verifica estoque, compras aprovam compra externa se necessário. Por que Flow Orchestration é indicado?
opcoes: Porque é apenas um processo batch simples
Porque coordena múltiplos passos humanos e automáticos ao longo do tempo
correta_texto: Porque coordena múltiplos passos humanos e automáticos ao longo do tempo

pergunta: Uma alternativa sugere usar apenas RPA para coordenar todas as aprovações humanas e sistemas em um processo de semanas. Qual é o problema com essa abordagem?
opcoes: Orchestration foi desenhado para coordenação de longo prazo e tarefas humanas em Salesforce
correta_texto: Orchestration foi desenhado para coordenação de longo prazo e tarefas humanas em Salesforce

pergunta: NTO quer decidir entre Flow Orchestration e um único Screen Flow para um processo de atendimento rápido que termina em poucos minutos em uma única sessão. O que a prova tende a preferir?
opcoes: Um Screen Flow simples, sem sobre‑engenharia
correta_texto: Um Screen Flow simples, sem sobre‑engenharia

pergunta: Uma questão oferece a opção de usar Orchestration para um cenário onde não há etapas humanas nem espera; tudo é síncrono e curto. Que tipo de pegadinha é essa?
correta_texto: Alternativa que adiciona complexidade desnecessária

pergunta: AnyAirlines quer que um gerente possa acompanhar o progresso de um processo complexo diretamente no registro de Conta. Qual combinação suporta isso?
opcoes: Orchestration + Work Guide exibido no registro
correta_texto: Orchestration + Work Guide exibido no registro

pergunta: Uma alternativa sugere duplicar a lógica de aprovação em vários Flows em vez de centralizá‑la em uma Orchestration e subflows. Por que isso é frágil?
opcoes: Porque Flows não podem ter lógica de aprovação
Porque duplica lógica e dificulta manutenção e auditoria
Porque Orchestration não pode chamar subflows
correta_texto: Porque duplica lógica e dificulta manutenção e auditoria

pergunta: NTO vê vários processos parados em estágios diferentes e precisa priorizar onde atuar. Qual artefato ajuda a ter essa visão de alto nível?
opcoes: Lista de Orchestration Runs com status (em andamento, pausada, falha, concluída)
Logs locais das máquinas dos usuários
correta_texto: Lista de Orchestration Runs com status (em andamento, pausada, falha, concluída)

pergunta: Uma questão mostra um processo em que, enquanto RH valida documentos, TI já pode começar a preparar o ambiente, ambos em paralelo. Qual resposta demonstra entendimento correto?
opcoes: Orchestration permite Steps paralelos em um Stage, concluído somente quando ambos terminam
Somente RPA pode rodar em paralelo
correta_texto: Orchestration permite Steps paralelos em um Stage, concluído somente quando ambos terminam

pergunta: Um candidato escolhe uma opção que diz: “em caso de erro, é melhor cancelar toda a Orchestration sem qualquer informação de estado”. Por que isso é inadequado?
opcoes: Porque perde rastreabilidade; melhor registrar, permitir retomada e manter histórico
correta_texto: Porque perde rastreabilidade; melhor registrar, permitir retomada e manter histórico

pergunta: AnyAirlines quer que processos de aprovação possam evoluir com o tempo, mantendo histórico de versões. Qual combinação está mais alinhada?
opcoes: Flows e Orchestrations versionados, com migração cuidadosa entre versões
correta_texto: Flows e Orchestrations versionados, com migração cuidadosa entre versões

pergunta: Uma alternativa sugere construir toda a lógica de processo em Apex e deixar Orchestration apenas como “fachada vazia”. Por que isso não reflete o foco do módulo?
opcoes: Porque Flow Orchestrator é justamente a camada para coordenar processo, e Apex deve ser usado apenas quando necessário
Porque Orchestration não chama Apex Actions
Porque a prova não considera Apex
correta_texto: Porque Flow Orchestrator é justamente a camada para coordenar processo, e Apex deve ser usado apenas quando necessário

pergunta: NTO quer usar Orchestration, mas também precisa chamar APIs MuleSoft em certos pontos do processo. Qual é a arquitetura recomendada?
opcoes: Orchestration chama Flows, que usam External Services/HTTP Callout para consumir APIs
correta_texto: Orchestration chama Flows, que usam External Services/HTTP Callout para consumir APIs

pergunta: Uma questão apresenta um processo que já está bem atendido por um Record‑Triggered Flow e algumas automações simples. Uma opção sugere migrar tudo para Orchestration “só porque é novo”. Como avaliar?
opcoes: Sempre migrar para a tecnologia mais nova
Reconhecer como pegadinha; só usar Orchestration quando houver benefício claro (longa duração, múltiplos times, etc.)
correta_texto: Reconhecer como pegadinha; só usar Orchestration quando houver benefício claro (longa duração, múltiplos times, etc.)

pergunta: AnyAirlines quer que, se a verificação de estoque via API falhar, o processo seja pausado e redirecionado para uma fila de análise. Como isso pode ser modelado?
opcoes: Background Step chamando Flow que trata falhas e encaminha Work Item para fila apropriada
correta_texto: Background Step chamando Flow que trata falhas e encaminha Work Item para fila apropriada

pergunta: Uma alternativa final afirma: “Flow Orchestration substitui completamente a necessidade de RPA, Composer e APIs em qualquer cenário de hiperautomação.” Qual resposta é mais alinhada?
opcoes: Concordar; Orchestration é suficiente para tudo
Discordar; Orchestration coordena processos, mas continua dependendo de Flows, APIs, RPA e Composer para executar tarefas especializadas
correta_texto: Discordar; Orchestration coordena processos, mas continua dependendo de Flows, APIs, RPA e Composer para executar tarefas especializadas
